Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:02 ET
P/C divergence signals dispersion trade as institutions hedge via index puts despite single-name bullishness
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence (index 1.69 puts-heavy vs single-name 0.64 calls-heavy) screams institutional dispersion hedge into GDP data.
  • 2. Positive $1.7B gamma environment creates compression risk — any breakout gets mechanically amplified beyond dealer hedging capacity.
  • 3. Vol surface contradictions in WMT, CSCO, PANW (rich vol/call heavy) suggest mispriced protection ahead of earnings cycle.
NARRATIVE — positive gamma compression trap

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices and single names — a classic institutional dispersion setup ahead of GDP data in 4 days. Index puts are 1.69x calls (heavy hedging) while single-name flow runs 0.64x (call-heavy), creating the widest spread in weeks as smart money hedges portfolio exposure while maintaining individual stock conviction. This structure aligns with the 0.04 implied correlation reading, confirming stock-specific regime where single-name vol trades more efficiently than index protection. Positive gamma totaling $1.7B across SPY/QQQ creates a compression trap that amplifies any directional break. With SPY trading exactly at both the put wall ($708) and gamma flip ($707.18), dealers are positioned to dampen moves — until they can't. The concentration of 1.4M SPY contracts clustered around current levels (707P-709C) means any GDP surprise triggers mechanical hedging beyond current capacity, particularly dangerous given 7 names in backwardation already pricing elevated near-term fear. Vol surface contradictions in mega-caps signal structural mispricing ahead of earnings. WMT, CSCO, and PANW show rich vol combined with call-heavy flow — historically a fade signal as elevated premium gets sold into demand. Meanwhile, Millennium's aggressive Q4 reversals in AMZN (+398%) and NVDA (+93%) provide conviction context for today's tech call flow, though their MSFT build (+135% to $2.49B) conflicts with the death cross technical signal.

Layer 1: Sentiment +55Layer 2: GEX +$1.7BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.69/0.64Layer 9: GDP in 4d
WMTVOL PLAY
Rich vol call-heavy fade
WMT shows rich vol/call heavy divergence — classic fade setup as elevated premium meets demand. +332% call volume (P/C 0.16) into vol trading rich vs sector, suggesting protection sellers will emerge ahead of earnings cycle.
L4: rich vol/call heavyL3: +332% call volL5: technical bullish
Sell WMT straddles, buy cheaper sector protection
USARVOL PLAY
Extreme two-way vol demand
USAR explodes +1025% calls (+452% puts) with P/C 0.16 — pure vol demand without directional bias. Two-way flow suggests event risk or corporate action not yet public, with vol buyers willing to pay any price for exposure.
L3: +1025% call volL3: +452% put volL8: no news catalyst
Long USAR straddles into unknown catalyst

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
